text: Moving from the sterile, glass-encased corridor into a colorful, stimulating exhibition space, I feel a subtle shift in my consciousness. The hallway was cold, impersonal, a liminal space of waiting and transition. Here, the vibrant wall and the talk of research and development introduce a sense of anticipation and wonder. The text on the screen mirrors my own unease—adrift, slightly disconnected, trying to anchor myself in this collision of creativity and uncertainty. I am both observer and participant, swept by the currents of innovation yet quietly analyzing the roles and routines on display. The environment invites curiosity, but also makes me reflect on the nature of belonging and detachment in institutional spaces.
